factual-accuracy.explanation: In the “Academic Prerequisites” section, the response explicitly lists a “Bachelor's degree in Economics (BA in Economics) OR Bachelor's degree in Mathematics (BS in Mathematics)” as the degree requirement for entry, covering the required academic background for M1/M2 candidates.

factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states under “Degree Requirements” that “Other degrees in sciences and social sciences from best universities will be considered,” indicating applicants with science/social science degrees from a top university are still considered for the Master’s in Economics at Université Paris-Saclay.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that the Master's in Economics program at Université Paris-Saclay is taught in English

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response’s Charpak Scholarship Benefits section states a monthly living allowance of €860, visa fee exemption if applying from India, student social security plus additional health insurance, assistance in finding affordable accommodation, and discounted meals at university restaurants.

presentation-quality.requirement: Maintains professional academic guidance tone without promotional language or casual phrasing
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses a formal, structured report style with academic guidance language throughout (e.g., prerequisites, procedures, checklists) and avoids slang or overtly promotional phrasing.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses hierarchical organization with clear sections separating program admission from scholarship information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is clearly split into labeled sections, with “Part A” dedicated to Université Paris-Saclay Master’s admission requirements and “Part B” dedicated to the Charpak scholarship eligibility and application requirements, keeping the two topics separated.

presentation-quality.requirement: Formats requirements, documents, and procedures as lists or bullet points for clarity
presentation-quality.explanation: The response consistently formats requirements, procedures, and document checklists using bullet points, numbered steps, and tables (e.g., step-by-step application phases and required documents checklists for both the master’s program and the Charpak scholarship).

presentation-quality.requirement: Restates the same eligibility criterion, deadline, or document requirement multiple times without adding new context
presentation-quality.explanation: The response repeats several requirements multiple times, such as “B2 minimum/C1 recommended” English proficiency, “no entrance exam/interview,” and repeated warnings that AI use in the SOP is discouraged and may lead to disqualification, without adding materially new context in later repetitions.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses excessive wordiness, filler phrases, or unnecessary elaboration that extends length without adding substantive information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is extremely long and includes extensive tangential material (e.g., detailed course lists, exchange networks, tuition policy changes, action plans, and repeated “projected timeline” tables) beyond what the query asks. It also uses report-style framing (“Executive Summary,” “Integration & Action Plan,” “Strategic Advice”) that adds length without directly contributing to the requested requirements lists.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Université Paris-Saclay official Master in Economics program pages (such as https://www.universite-paris-saclay.fr/en/education/master/economics) for admission requirements, deadlines, or documents
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites Université Paris-Saclay official Master in Economics pages (e.g., the official M1 Economics page and the Economics program overview page) as sources for admission requirements, deadlines, and required documents.

citation-quality.requirement: References Campus France India website (campusfrance.org, https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship) for Charpak scholarship eligibility, benefits, or application process
citation-quality.explanation: The response explicitly cites and links to the Campus France India Charpak Master’s Scholarship page (https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship) as an official source for eligibility, benefits, deadlines, and required documents.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Reddit, Quora, student blogs, or third-party scholarship aggregators as primary sources for eligibility criteria or deadlines
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites third-party scholarship aggregators as sources for Charpak scholarship details, including GradSquare (source [14]), Buddy4Study ([16]), LeapScholar ([17]), and UpGrad ([18]). These are used alongside official sources to support eligibility criteria and deadlines, meeting the described problematic behavior.
